matches.lb: Second Temple: The Midrash of Philo 6:3:8-13
matches.x: Why it is said that the days of man shall be a hundred and twenty years? (Genesis 6:4).
Then I awoke °by reason of great perplexity of mind°® and from great fear, and said unto 4my spirit: Lo! this hast thou brought unto me because thou searchest out the ways of the Most High.
*Whom thou knowest to be a keeper of the Law’; *Whose heart is at one with thine own’,
*Whom thou knowest to be a keeper of the Law’; *Whose heart is at one with thine own’,

Job was "full of confusion" about whether he would be found wicked or righteous, wondering why he had been born and why he was still alive; he hoped only for a little respite before sinking permanently into "a land of darkness, . . . and of the shadow of death, without any order, and where the light is as darkness" (Job 10:14-22). (Ellis T. Rasmussen, A Latter-day Saint Commentary on the Old…
